context: Post owned-attention + FO2 + Q4K_GEMV_WARP: weight-GEMV at llama parity, attention near-parity. tinygrad ~85-88% of llama @ctx1024 (wall gap +1.73ms/tok). Residual = KV-copy lifecycle + unfused small-ops/activation.

ranking.expected_wd_if_solved: ~+5-9% (gqa-era MAXC-shrink +8 tok/s; needs re-confirm on owned route)
ranking.boundedness: bounded (single materialization; owned-tile blocker now fixed)
ranking.first_gate: DIAGNOSTIC: (a) MAXC-shrink A/B on owned route confirms >=5% wall; (b) opaque append + fixed owned tile + fp16 cache is byte-identical multi-step. Then implement.

ranking.lane: small-ops / norm / rope / FFN-activation FUSION (native codegen)
ranking.expected_wd_if_solved: uncertain -- largest GPU-busy gap but heavily overlapped; wall transfer unproven
ranking.first_gate: measure which small-ops are on the WALL critical path (not just GPU-busy); prototype ONE fusion (e.g. silu into the FFN GEMV epilogue) and check W==D transfer before broad work
only if a cheap extension of the existing Q4K_GEMV_WARP to q/o proj shows a local win
this audit proved llvm-objdump/readelf on hipcc code objects works; package it

decision_rationale: Runtime-KV/KV-copy is the most BOUNDED, IMPACT-JUSTIFIED, and on-critical-path residual (the small-ops gap is bigger in GPU-busy but heavily overlapped and codegen-unbounded). The prior runtime-KV blocker was entangled with the now-fixed owned-tile dtype bug, so a fresh cheap diagnostic is warranted before the hard implementation. If the diagnostic refutes the wall impact or re-confirms the append-NaN, fall back to NEXT_PRIMITIVE_SMALL_OPS_Q8 (fusion).
note: No NO_BOUNDED_8B_PRIMITIVE_REMAINS -- runtime-KV remains bounded and impact-justified. But the project is now at ~85-88% of llama with attention+GEMV solved; remaining wins are smaller and lifecycle/codegen-bound. Generalizing the promoted routes to other models/shapes (NEXT_PROJECT_GENERALIZE) is a reasonable strategic alternative to further 8B grinding.
